Wish list
RW Reviews
Important Shipping Note: U.S. sales only.

Here at Running Warehouse, we want you to be completely satisfied with your purchase. If you are unsatisfied with your purchase for any reason, items can be returned with free return shipping within 90 days from the original invoice date.

We will match or beat any posted overall price advertised in-store or online on in stock items.

  • Overview
    Overview Specs & Fit Technology Videos Reviews
  • Overview
  • Specs & Fit
  • Technologies
  • Videos
  • Reviews
  • Buy

The ASICS Novablast has quickly ascended to the top of the daily training category thanks to its versatile, do-it-all performance. Version 5 is designed to be the softest and fastest version yet.

Best Use

Daily TrainersDaily Trainers

Surface

Roads & PavementRoads & Pavement

Stability

Neutral
Stable

Cushioning

Minimal
Low
Medium
High
Maximal

All Men's Road Shoes

  • Daily TrainersDaily Trainers

    As the broad base of the running shoe pyramid, daily trainers are the definition of a go-to running shoe. They prioritize dependable comfort, support, durability, and routine versatility. They're a staple in every runner's closet. If you're newer to running shoes, you can confidently start your journey here.

  • Performance TrainersPerformance Trainers

    Performance trainers (or "super trainers") blend elements of daily trainers and super shoes, often giving you the benefits of advanced foams and plates while being adapted for daily training. They make fast runs faster and long runs easier. Their high versatility also makes them great choices as accessible racing shoes.

  • Super ShoesSuper Shoes

    The pinnacle of the pyramid, super shoes are the fastest running shoes you can buy. They're designed to race your absolute best and are the apex of running-shoe technology and innovation. Often built with carbon-fiber plates and cutting-edge superfoams for the highest energy return, they're made for ultimate racing speed.

Men's Running Shoes by Surface

  • Roads & PavementRoads & Pavement

    Road running shoes feature smooth outsoles that are designed for running on paved surfaces such as roads, sidewalks, and bike paths.

  • Smooth Trails & FireroadsSmooth Trails & Fireroads

    Designed to handle most trail runs, these shoes prioritize comfort and a smooth ride. These shoes are great for anything from smooth singletrack, park trails, and fireroads making them ideal for those who run from their doorstep on streets before hitting the trail.

  • Rocky & Technical TrailsRocky & Technical Trails

    These shoes are best used for hard, rugged trails such as shale, granite or sandstone where grip on smooth surfaces and underfoot protection are important.

  • Soft & Muddy TrailsSoft & Muddy Trails

    Designed for use in muddy, soggy conditions, these shoes feature very aggressive outsoles that dig deep into soft ground for exceptional traction.

  • Snowy & Icy TrailsSnowy & Icy Trails

    These shoes feature technical outsoles designed to grip snowy and icy trails making them ideal for winter trail running.

Cushioning Level

  • Cushioning level, or stack height, refers to how much shoe is between your foot and the ground. For this category, we reference the amount of cushioning below the forefoot as the heel height will be equal to or greater than the forefoot height.
    Learn more about stack heights and how we measure them.

  • Minimal

    14-18mm. The shoe has a thin midsole that allows for a natural running experience. Racing shoes and minimalist shoes are common here. These shoes offer a feeling of being connected to the road or trail.

  • Low

    19-23mm. The shoe has a slightly cushioned feel and may feature added cushioning technologies. Performance training shoes and some trail shoes are common here. These offer protection during footstrike but prioritize a lightweight, grounded experience.

  • Medium

    24-28mm. These shoes have a stack height that fall near the middle of the spectrum.The shoes in this category are verstaile and great for all types of runs and distances.

  • High

    29-34mm. The shoe has a thick midsole and ample cushioning. These shoes are highly protective and absorb more impact than the body.

  • Maximal

    35mm plus. The shoe has an extremely thick midsole and extra cushioning. The focus is on protection and soft foam underfoot with hardly any ground feel.

Stability Level

  • Neutral Running Shoes

    Neutral shoes support the foot through a normal range of arch collapse and generally do not have built-in technology to correct movement.

  • Stability Running Shoes

    Stability shoes are a great option for those who overpronate or need added support. These shoes help to limit the inward rolling motion of the ankle while running or walking and assist in guiding the foot straight through the gait cycle.


At A Glance:

  • Plush energy
  • Uncomplicated effectiveness
  • Preeminent versatility

Product Highlights:

The ASICS Novablast is a multi-talented running shoe that has swiftly become one of our bestsellers and one of the most popular shoes in the running world, with no signs of slowing down. The Novablast 5 delivers exciting new updates with a softer foam, a premium new upper, and an enhanced rockered geometry, for a comfortable performance ride no matter the type of run.

Featuring a full layer of plush, light, and bouncy FF BLAST MAX for daily training, the Novablast 5 brings softer landings and a more responsive toe-off than previous versions. Protecting the midsole is a proprietary AHAR LO rubber outsole for a soft but durable grip.

Wrapping it all up is a sleek new engineered jacquard mesh upper that offers more stretch, ventilation, and durability. No matter the distance, speed, or workout, the ASICS Novablast 5 performs well in almost any circumstance. If you want a multi-skilled daily trainer that leads the pack, look no further.

Model Number: 1011B974.020

Specs & FitLearn More

Shoe Specs

  • Weight: 8.7 oz | 247 g
  • Heel Stack: 41 mm
  • Forefoot Stack: 33 mm
  • Heel-Toe Offset: 8 mm

How It FitsLearn More

Heel Width

Narrow
Snug
Average
Roomy
Wide

Midfoot Fit

Narrow
Snug
Average
Roomy
Wide

Toe Box Fit

Narrow
Snug
Average
Roomy
Wide

How We Measure Our Shoe Specs

  • We measure every shoe in-house to provide accurate and consistent details.

  • Weight

    The weight is based on a Men's size 9 for Men's and Unisex shoes or Women's size 8 for Women's shoes. The weight listed is for a single shoe, including the laces.

  • Heel Stack

    The heel stack refers to how high your heel sits above the ground when wearing shoes. Based on Men's size 9 as the standard.

  • Forefoot Stack

    Forefoot stack refers to how high the ball of the foot sits above the ground when wearing shoes. Based on Men's size 9 as the standard.

  • Heel-toe Offset

    Heel-toe offset (drop) is the difference in height between the heel stack and the forefoot stack. We use the number given to us by the brand.

How We Measure Shoe Fit

  • All measurements are based on Medium width. Note: fit is relative to the "average" running shoe.

  • We partner with SmartLast, a program by Heeluxe, to use advanced shoe-measuring technology, to provide objective measurements for toe box, midfoot, and heel widths as well as overall length.

Cushioning Level

  • MinimalMinimal

    14-18mm. The shoe has a thin midsole that allows for a natural running experience. Racing shoes and minimalist shoes are common here. These shoes offer a feeling of being connected to the road or trail.

  • LowLow

    19-23mm. The shoe has a slightly cushioned feel and may feature added cushioning technologies. Performance training shoes and some trail shoes are common here. These offer protection during footstrike but prioritize a lightweight, grounded experience.

  • MediumMedium

    24-28mm. These shoes have a stack height that fall near the middle of the spectrum.The shoes in this category are verstaile and great for all types of runs and distances.

  • HighHigh

    29-34mm. The shoe has a thick midsole and ample cushioning. These shoes are highly protective and absorb more impact than the body.

  • MaximalMaximal

    35mm plus. The shoe has an extremely thick midsole and extra cushioning. The focus is on protection and soft foam underfoot with hardly any ground feel.

Technologies

MIDSOLE

  • FLYTEFOAM BLAST MAX is ASICS' lightest and most responsive daily training foam (FF LEAP and FF TURBO formulations for racing remain their most responsive foams).

OUTSOLE

  • AHAR LO rubber is a lower-hardness version of ASICS High Abrasion Resistant rubber covering the outsole for advanced durability.
  • Trampoline-inspired geometry creates a unique ride with more responsive bounce.

UPPER

  • Engineered jacquard mesh offers more stretch, ventilation, and durability, as well as boasting a main material that's 75% recycled.
  • Tongue wing construction improves fit and reduces movement.

Videos

Customer Reviews

3.9 out of 5
25 Customer Reviews
Showing
All Star ReviewsClear
1 out of 5 Stars
not worth the moneygreat shoe in the beginning, but the foam in the heel fails quickly for a $150.00 shoe. Foam failed before having 200 miles on the shoe. No help from ASIC to address the failing foam. Waste of money.
Anonymous - OK, United StatesFebruary 25, 2026
5 out of 5 Stars
Would Recommend
750+ Miles Logged and Loved Ever StepI'm no running shoe expert but I have put over 750 miles on these in the past year and they have held up great, kept me injury free, and been wonderfully snappy, cushy, and stylish the whole time. To each their own but I loved this shoe and am buying another pair.
Jake - UT, United StatesFebruary 09, 2026
5 out of 5 Stars
Would Recommend
The Mileage EatersI just finished my marathon training season with these as my primary training shoes. I put over 350 miles in them, and did a fair mix of speed training, tempos, and long runs. These shoes are deceptively fast for how soft they feel, and they really shine on the long runs. I would not recommend them as a track shoe as there are other shoes that are cheaper and more suitable to that (brooks launch). For that 7-10 mile distance where most marathon trainers shine, these are slightly better than the New Balance Rebel v4s I tested because of the spring and downhill running feel. In my long runs, these were my go-to because they felt nice and light for how much cushion they brought. They are very comfortable out of the box and broke in well and never lost their spring. Some may not like the trampoliney feel but I found them very stable for how high they ride.
Cole H - CA, United StatesJanuary 16, 2026
[This review was collected as part of a promotion.]
5 out of 5 Stars
Would Recommend
Love 'emSuper comfy shoes: the cushioning is amazing. I wear them for all my easy and distance runs and they’ve done well on fartleks too. Perfect do-it-all trainer for my rotation. I’m using them as my everyday shoes in the off season as well.
Matt - IL, United StatesNovember 23, 2025
5 out of 5 Stars
Would Recommend
Buy These for XC SZNI am a high school senior running about 50 miles a week and these shoes carried me during XC season. I would highly recommend. I used these for my long runs (usually about 12ish miles) and easy runs (7-8 miles) . Wouldn't really use these for anything faster than 6:30 pace other than strides, but they were great for the use that I got them for.
Tommy - KY, United StatesNovember 16, 2025
2 out of 5 Stars
UnforgivingCut a piece of 2x4 to the length of your foot, add a strip of rubber to the bottom, an inch of foam to the top and secure on your foot with loose sandal straps, then go for a run. This is what this shoe felt like to me. Really hard on ankles and shins.
Ted - NM, United StatesOctober 10, 2025
[This review was collected as part of a promotion.]
3 out of 5 Stars
I really like the shoe, but!Five stars for Running Warehouse, but 3-1/2-ish stars for the Novablast 5s. I ran 5 in them on Thursday, steady state with 5 x 0:20 strides. My take then, "I like these shoes!" I got them for non-carbon long run shoes. So, I was looking forward to running in them this morning (08/23/25). The first 5 felt just like Thursday, but then the slipping start. Here's the catch, I live on the Gulf of Mexico, in Mississippi, and my sweat rate is ~1 LB per 15 minutes. By mile 6 the shoes were soaked with sweat, and the sweat was running out onto the outer sole which was causing the slippage. Also, I was slipping around inside the shoes. The final 7 miles was a no-traction and weird slipping around struggle. I usually run in Puma Deviate Nitros, Adidas Bostons, Nike Invincibles, and this has never happened before with those shoes. So, the tread on these shoes do not like damp conditions, and the interior materials kind of act like sponges. Long story short, while I'm tempted to return them, I'll save them for shorter recovery runs and longer runs when it cools off down here.
Andy S. - MS, United StatesAugust 23, 2025
5 out of 5 Stars
Would Recommend
Love this shoeI love this shoe, right out the box, hopped on the treadmill 10/10 great feel and awesome shoe.
Steezy - TX, United StatesJuly 24, 2025
[This review was collected as part of a promotion.]
5 out of 5 Stars
Would Recommend
Got it right againI loved the Novablast 1's, 2's and 3's hated the 4's (too puffy and no pick up). Love the 5's. Perfect fit, very comfortable and good energy return.
Sue P - UT, United StatesJuly 03, 2025
5 out of 5 Stars
Would Recommend
One of the best shoes I've ever hadI've been running for 20+ years. As I get older, I need more support. This shoe has been perfect, including getting through a half marathon.
David H. - VA, United StatesJune 11, 2025
2 out of 5 Stars
Mushy and sloppy.
Elias Cabrera Munoz - CA, United StatesJune 01, 2025
1 out of 5 Stars
Way Too Soft FoamThe first couple of runs in these were outstanding. Then, around mile 25, the foam collapsed on the inner right side of my left shoe, rendering the shoe basically unusable, even for walking. This is my second attempt at wearing Novablasts; the first was with the 4s, which were fun, but the bevel was too much and tripped me up on long runs. Never again.
Anonymous - VA, United StatesApril 12, 2025
4 out of 5 Stars
Would Recommend
Endorphin speed 3 replacementFor context: 5.7 distance runner with 5 years running experience; mid foot striker with high cadence; 175 lbs. 110 miles and this shoe still sings. Great for anything EZ pace to Marathon pace so I use it primarily for my EZ runs and long run, which is 10-12 miles atm. Strides are fun in it too. Fits TTS. Have not been a fan of the recent FF blast iterations but this FF Blast Max midsole is definitely an intriguing EVA. It’s soft yet bouncy and resilient. I don’t get sensation of running in sand like I do with the recent 1080 and Gel nimbus. The outsole is much improved as well from previous Novablasts. No slipping in rainy runs on concrete for me and handles buffed out trails and fire roads just fine. Upper is fantastic. Everything about the upper says premium, even the laces are great. The only reason I’m not giving it 5 stars is because PUMAGRIP exists and the naming of ASICS outsole compounds is annoying. I’ll say one thing on the stability: this is a true neutral shoe again and I think most people who experienced pain in this shoe have been running in stable neutral shoes for a while. My first few runs gave me some new mild aches and pains but that’s likely due to me running in the Superblast 1 and Speed 4s for the last few months. I adapted just fine tho and my legs feel great after every run in these now. In summary, this is a phenomenal update from ASICS that brings a ton of bouncy fun and comfort at great price. I’m not sure what the future holds for EVA foams but this one can hang with the more modern foams if you ask me.
Lance - WV, United StatesMarch 15, 2025
3 out of 5 Stars
Not this year…I’ve been a long time Novablast runner, but this iteration is causing me to look elsewhere. For context, I have a narrow and somewhat low volume foot. I think the 5’s became noticeably wider than the 4’s. I’ve noticed some consistent irritation in the distal attachment of my anterior tibialis tendon due to the need to crank on the laces to feel mildly secure in the shoe. I feel that the addition of a runner’s knot has minimal improvement to this problem, and is something I’m trying to avoid as the tongue has been shortened a good bit as well. Negative points aside, I do think the new midsole foam has a good feel but won’t be getting long term durability testing out of the shoe to confirm how well it holds up. Can’t confirm but I would speculate that this still is a nice daily option for a runner with wider feet.
Zach - MN, United StatesMarch 11, 2025
[This review was collected as part of a promotion.]
1 out of 5 Stars
The worst trainer I’ve ever ran in.Can’t even call this shoe a trainer, it’s more like a Chinese finger trap and rock tumbler for your feet. I put less than 50 miles on them and my ankles are feeling pain that I haven’t felt in my entire life. If you are a mid-foot striker, easy runs will be no problem but long runs will hurt after 6+ miles. If you are a forefoot striker, like myself, be prepared to start a gofundme for ankle surgery. Everything good about the Novablast 4 was stripped away in the 5, it’s not the same shoe at all, the 4 is the greatest trainer ever created and the 5 is a torture device. The snappiness is gone in this new model. The foam is too soft so that it’s not even a high cushion shoe at all. The toe box and heel are both way too roomy. This is a walking shoe, at best. The only good update about this shoe is the laces, only because the laces for the 4 were so bad. ASICS, please do better. If you’re expecting anything out of the 5 that’s remotely close to the 4, just stop. The 5 is as if I had a fever dream about the 4 and created the new model based on that myself. Don’t buy this shoe. Hopefully the 6 is better.
Stephan C. - CA, United StatesMarch 10, 2025
[This review was collected as part of a promotion.]
2 out of 5 Stars
Over HypedI've been running in the Superblast V1. Thought I would save $60 and got the Novablast. I find them wildly unstable. The wide toe box allowed my forefoot to move medially into the shoe and the super plush cushioning lacks any arch support. The second run in them led to strain on lower lateral leg. I don't see how so many people could hype this shoe when it's clearly lacking in even the slightest support.
Aj - OH, United StatesMarch 07, 2025
5 out of 5 Stars
Would Recommend
Noblest 5Están muy cómodos (They are very comfortable)
Berenice Samaniego - TX, MexicoFebruary 02, 2025
[This review was collected as part of a promotion.]
4 out of 5 Stars
Would Recommend
I returned shoe but did not agree with meI want to be clear, this is a great shoe, but not for me. I think it looks great, It is designed great and perfectly cushioned, but not for me. When I walk on the shoe, wow great love it. Running at a 10 minute mile it is slappy and i get minimal cushion. It just is flat. If I ramp up to a 7-8 minute mile, clearly my form improves and it is not slappy. I feel somewhat more protective. This tells me two things and I appreciate this. 1. My form breaks down at slower speeds. I need to fix my form. 2. This is not the right shoe for my easy pace. I can see most runners loving this and I think it is a great shoe. It honestly loses 1 point as a reminder that not every shoe is for everyone. i am 5'10 180lb male and I think it will work for alot of heavier runners like myself. If you are in the 12 minute mile pace, Asics has better offerings, this is still more of a performance shoe than say a nimbus or cumulus. I would treat this only as a speed day shoe if you needed that.
Jay - NJ, United StatesJanuary 24, 2025
[This review was collected as part of a promotion.]
5 out of 5 Stars
Would Recommend
Felt like the Novablast 2'sIn recent new novablast shoes I have not been a fan of how it felt like I was being forced to my forefront as much as the 2's did. The 2's were less extreme than the 4's, I cannot speak on the 3's. All that being said the 5's feel similar to the 2's in the sense as it does not push you on your forefront as much as the 4's. I got over 600 miles out of the 2's and the 5's I now have I hope to get to 500 or so. The shoe has a great rock to it and I have used it for daily miles as well as long runs with some hard miles in it.
Will - MD, United StatesJanuary 17, 2025
[This review was collected as part of a promotion.]
5 out of 5 Stars
Would Recommend
Great all-around shoeFeels great right out of the box. While v4 was good, v5 is better, especially in the cushioning category. True to size with a spacious but not loose fit. Good traction when it's wet. Soft and resilient ride without feeling like it's bottoming out or too mushy.
Bruce S - OH, United StatesJanuary 01, 2025
[This review was collected as part of a promotion.]
5 out of 5 Stars
Super pleasedAdded these to my rotation and I’m ordering a second pair. Protective, lightweight and responsive. Softer than the Saucony Speed 4’s but not squishy. Much softer and smoother than the Puma Deviate 3’s. They are slightly wide on my narrower foot, but all in all I am super pleased.
Dave - FL, United StatesDecember 30, 2024
[This review was collected as part of a promotion.]
5 out of 5 Stars
Slightly better than Novablast 4If you like NB 4, you will like NB 5. However, it runs about 1/2 size large. I wore a 11.5 wide in the NB 4 and the same size in NB 5 is too long.
Anonymous - OH, United StatesDecember 14, 2024
[This review was collected as part of a promotion.]
4 out of 5 Stars
Would Recommend
So Far So Good....As advertised, it's really versatile, very light weight, great cushion, fairly responsive. My only complaint is that with the elevated stack height the proportion of cushion is heavily weighted to the back of the shoe - great for heel strikers, but when I picked up the pace to a tempo+ effort, the midfoot cushion leaves a bit to be desired. With that being said, it's a great, all-around, versatile shoe that can be used for all types of runs.
Anonymous - CA, United StatesDecember 09, 2024
[This review was collected as part of a promotion.]
5 out of 5 Stars
Would Recommend
Clean!!!These are the perfect amount of soft and smooth ride for all those easy day milers. Highly recommend but that’s just me haha
Anonymous - CA, United StatesDecember 06, 2024
[This review was collected as part of a promotion.]
5 out of 5 Stars
Would Recommend
The 5 Rocks !I’ve run in every version of the NovaBlast and the “5” puts Fun back in the shoe ! The “4” was a dud to me . The “5” is lively and Fun to run in .
Bob - TX , United StatesDecember 05, 2024
[This review was collected as part of a promotion.]
